message.content:
<deliberation>
Step-by-step approach:
- Parsed main_entry_nodes: REG-091, REG-051, REG-025.
- Located main_nodes content for REG-051 (AI Act Art26(9)) and REG-025 (AI Act Art13(1)). No content was provided for REG-091.
- Collected cross-references from referenced_nodes for Article 13: REG-026 through REG-032 (AI Act Art13(2)–(8)).
- Interpreted regulatory context:
  - REG-051: Requires deployers of high-risk AI systems to use the Article 13 information to comply with GDPR DPIA obligations (GDPR Art 35). Cross-references include Article 13 details (REG-026–REG-032). Full text of GDPR Art 35 was not provided; per instructions, if not available, omissions cannot be treated as deviations.
  - REG-025: Requires transparency/interpretability for high-risk AI systems and links to obligations elaborated across Art13(2)–(8) (REG-026–REG-032).
  - REG-091: No regulatory text provided; cannot extract quotes; must classify as Non-Deviation.
- Reviewed the policy chunk for alignment/contradictions:
  - The policy describes internal review processes (Restricted Uses, Sensitive Uses), Impact Assessment templates, reporting to an Office of Responsible AI, and annual review. It does not explicitly contradict or alter the regulatory requirements of REG-051 or REG-025; mainly omissions (no explicit mention of Article 13-based instructions for use, interpretability mandates, or GDPR DPIA under Art 35).
- Per rule 4 on omissions and rule 4.1 on evidence, no deviations can be asserted without explicit contradictory content and exact regulatory quotes. Therefore, all three nodes result in Non-Deviation findings.
- Included considered_references for nodes that refer to Article 13 (REG-051 and REG-025). For REG-091, no text or references available, so none listed.
</deliberation>
